title: ICT Technician
description: ICT Technician Jamaica Tertiary Education Commission Kingston and St. Andrew See description Fixed term contract Updated 16/02/2026 HRM Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save Maintains in-house technology equipment and ICT assets.  This includes installing, configuring, diagnosing, repairing, and upgrading all Agency hardware and equipment while ensuring their optimal performance. Jamaica Tertiary Education Commission JOB DESCRIPTION JOB TITLE: ICT Technician JOB GRADE: MIS/IT 3 DIVISION (SECTION): CORPORATE SERVICES (ICT Section) REPORTS TO: Manager, ICT SUPERVISES DIRECTLY: Not Applicable Job Purpose : The ICT Technician (Hardware) maintains in-house technology equipment and ICT assets.  This includes installing, configuring, diagnosing, repairing, and upgrading all Agency hardware and equipment while ensuring their optimal performance. The incumbent will also troubleshoot problem areas in a timely and accurate manner, and provide end user training and assistance where required. Key Outputs: Current preventative maintenance schedules On-time response to resolve technology issues Operational technology devises and equipment Configured computers, communication devises and peripheral equipment End User training in productivity tools delivered Up-to-date Hardware supplies inventory Key Duties and Responsibilities Technical responsibilities : Install, test and maintain a variety of personal computing and network hardware systems; perform diagnostic tests, modify or repair hardware to the board or component level (while honouring the warranty or legal restrictions on the equipment); gather and record appropriate data related to these functions. Perform post installation checkout, operations and maintenance support. Configures computers, communications devices and peripheral equipment. Build and assemble interconnecting cables and other specialised hardware. Install hardware, cables and related devices. Coordinate personal computer hardware and software troubleshooting, installation, repair, problem diagnosis, and implementation. Perform on-site analysis, diagnosis, and resolution of complex PC problems for a variety of end users, and recommend and implement corrective hardware solutions, including off-site repair as needed. Receive calls for assistance, installations, and general maintenance; logs calls and takes appropriate action to ensure a satisfactory response within the established timeframe for the user community; provides management and status reports on all requests. Liaise with, and provide training and support to, end users and staff on the use of productivity devises and machinery as well as computer operation and other issues as required or instructed Maintain a complete inventory of supplies and parts and request replacements at required reorder levels in a timely manner. Utilise personal computing hardware and software word-processing, spreadsheet, and database applications to perform basic duties including documentation, reporting, scheduling and user support. Perform any other related duties as required. Administrative Responsibilities : Recommend and coordinate periodic cleaning and maintenance schedule for Agency’s computers, printers and photocopiers; Liaise with third-party support and PC equipment vendors as required. Administer the maintenance/servicing of machines and print management systems. This includes changing license on software and updating PC with relevant software. Administer the print management group by assigning or removing users from print management groups. Examine devices, record the status of service/repairs and request repairs or disposal according to policy. Assess areas for new equipment to supply the specifications/workload of the areas. Prepare and submits relevant schedules and reports as required Authority to: Recommend/monitor periodic cleaning and maintenance schedule for Agency’s computers, printers and photocopiers Recommend the issuing of replacement/interim devices Assist in reviewing work peripheral devises and the work of contractors on such devises for conformance to standard Install hardware devices as per approval Recommend repairs Fix/repair devices Decisions referred to immediate supervisor Approval for the repair/replacement/purchase of productivity devices by vendors. Approval of any travel. Performance Standards: Productivity devices are operational. Respond to Help Desk tickets and other queries within established turnaround times. Down time of devices is within the stipulated ceiling. Users are sensitised to the use of productivity devices. Departmental policies, procedures, standards, and ICT controls adhered to. Confidentiality, integrity and professionalism are displayed in the execution of all duties and in personal conduct. Working Conditions: Normal office environment. Possibility of being required to work at the weekends, for extended hours/at night and on public holidays to facilitate cleaning of machinery or emergencies. Ability to lift and carry at least 50lbs (ICT related equipment/PC devices) May be required to travel to external locations in support of JTEC activities. Required Competencies: Core Ability to communicate effectively both orally and in writing Sound knowledge of computer configurations and network operations. Good critical thinking, problems solving and decision making skills Sound spatial competencies Good human relations/customer service ability Quick learner Technical Good understanding of various operating systems, Internal and External Contacts Within Jamaica Tertiary Education Commission Contact (Title) Purpose of Communication Manager, ICT To receive instructions and guidance. Staff and Members of the Commission To provide and/or obtain information and for coordinating activities and technical support as required. Staff To resolve relevant issues and provide training. Contacts external to the organisation required for the achievement of the position objectives Contact (Title) Purpose of Communication Suppliers of goods and services To effect repairs or acquire information. External institutions To secure information required to implement solutions in support of JTEC activities. Minimum Required Education and Experience BSc. Degree in Computer Science from a recognised tertiary institution 1 year’s experience in a related field OR Diploma in Computer Science or Computer Engineering or equivalent qualifications. At least 2 years’ experience in an IT environment with experience with installation and maintenance of personal computers, operating systems and application software. OR Certification in Computer Hardware, Repair and Troubleshooting (CompTIA A+) with 4-5 years’ experience in an IT environment installing, maintaining personal computers, printers, operating systems and application software Proven ability to communicate effectively both orally and in writing Exceptional customer orientation. Ref: ICTTech Apply Now

title: Development Support Analyst – Insurance Application Services
description: Development Support Analyst – Insurance Application Services Sagicor Group Jamaica Ltd. Kingston and St. Andrew Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 16/02/2026 SGJ TAU Log in or register to apply Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save While we appreciate all applicants, only applications received via Sagicor's career portal https://career4.successfactors.com/career?company=sagicorfinP2 will be considered. "Looking for a diverse and rewarding career? If you're looking to grow your career within a stable and growing financial services company, then Sagicor may be right for you!" Sagicor Group Jamaica Limited is seeking a suitable candidate to join our Group Application Services team in the capacity of: Development Support Analyst – Insurance Application Services Investigate, troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to software applications and systems. Work closely with development teams, stakeholders, and end-users to identify and fix problems, ensuring minimal downtime and optimal system performance. Location: Kingston As a Development Support Analyst – Insurance Application Services, you will: Evaluate and undertake impact analysis on major technical options and takes a leading role in proposing technical solutions within area of expertise. Take responsibility for investigative work to seek effective business solutions for assigned projects. Utilize business experience and skills to assess and advise on the practicability of alternatives. Take responsibility as needed for the detailed specification and modelling of recommended solutions. Plan, arrange and control meetings, workshops, and relations with client/user staff during system investigations and throughout subsequent development work. Take technical responsibility for most stages of the life cycle for software development of all types of systems. Prescribe standards for the terms and symbols used in program documentation. Assist to set guidelines for the preparation of policies and procedures manuals. Coordinate the preparation of detailed documentation on the operation of programs for users and computer operators. Undertake all work in accordance with required technical and quality standards and using appropriate methods and tools to achieve well-engineered product. Recommend program changes to increase operating efficiency or to adapt to new systems requirements. Modify application software to include corrective maintenance. Implement version control and release management procedures, as per change management policy. Apply automated and manual tools including ‘black box’ and ‘white box’ testing, to test and evaluate systems and networks under development. Monitor all activities against plan, providing regular and accurate reports to senior management, and client/user management as appropriate. Maintain contact with larger software and hardware vendors for support and to remain informed of developments within the industry. Communicate with core software vendors, to develop system designs into specific functionality requirements. Perform other job-related duties assigned from time to time. What do you need? Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent qualification from a recognized tertiary institution. A minimum of two (1) years working experience in IT related disciplines. Strong analytical/critical thinking skills. Results oriented. Effective interpersonal and communication skills. A team player. Sound decision-making skills. Working knowledge of programming languages, such as COBOL, TSO, JCSL, CICS C#, SQL, CSS, HTML, JavaScript, DB2 and MSSQL. Familiarity with Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) methodologies. Ability to design and develop moderately complex programs and programs modification. Experience in application systems integration of average complexity. If this role is of interest to you, kindly submit an application via Sagicor's career portal no later than February 20, 2026. While we appreciate all applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Ref: Development Support Analyst – Insurance Applicatio Log in or register to apply

title: Chief Operations Officer at Apex Radiology
description: Chief Operations Officer at Apex Radiology Great People Solutions (G.P.S.) Limited Kingston and St. Andrew Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 17/02/2026 GPS HR Director Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save Chief Operations Officer – Apex Radiology Chief Operations Officer – Apex Radiology Are you a bold, visionary leader with high potential to take the helm of a market-leading healthcare organization? Our Client, Apex Radiology, is seeking a Chief Operations Officer (COO) who isn't just looking for a job, but a development opportunity. We need a high-confidence leader with a deep ownership mindset and a people-centered approach to drive our next chapter of growth. If you possess the blend of being a champion for care and excellence while having financial acumen, and a heart for people-first leadership we invite you to apply. About the Company Image Plus Consultants Limited , trading as Apex Radiology , is a premier name in Jamaican medical diagnostic imaging. With 30 years of excellence and an official listing on the Jamaica Stock Exchange (JSE) Junior Market , Apex has built a reputation for high-quality, accessible care. Operating across four locations with revenues exceeding $1B , Apex is recognized for its stellar corporate governance and commitment to cutting-edge services. The company is entering a phase of strategic expansion while streamlining operations to redefine service delivery in the region. About the Role Reporting directly to the CEO, the COO is a pivotal executive responsible for translating futuristic strategies into world-class operational reality. You will oversee the daily heartbeat of the company, ensuring synergy across all branch locations. Key responsibilities include: Strategy & Performance: Developing strategic and operational plans and managing branch budgets, forecasts, and KPIs/SLAs to ensure organizational alignment. Operational and Customer Excellence: Managing day to day operations while maintaining futuristic operating guidelines designed to ensure efficiency, exceptional medical care, and maximum machine uptime. Facilities Management, Procurement and Audits: Managing facilities and key support functions end-to-end to ensure proper procurement processes and management controls are in place and well managed at all times. Continuous Improvement: Identifying and acting upon opportunities to improve efficiency through automation, standardization, and the strategic use of AI. People Leadership & Development: Fostering a healthy sense of team spirit and unity while providing direct oversight to senior roles, including the Bio-Med/IT Support Manager, Operations Manager, and Branch Supervisors. Growth & Coaching: Facilitating a collaborative environment where team members are continuously trained and empowered to grow in their roles. Relationship Management and Executive Oversight: Being a trusted partner to the CEO, building robust stakeholder relationships, preparing reports for the Board of Directors and providing leadership for the company in the absence of the CEO. About the Person We are looking for a tech-savvy, critical thinker who inspires and motivates others to achieve greatness. You must be a "people person" who finds joy in building high-performing teams and delivering results. Key Competencies: Education & Experience: A Master’s degree in Business Administration, Logistics, or a related field. You should have at least 5 years of experience in Operations management, with at least 3 years at a senior leadership level. Industry Knowledge: While work experience in medical administration is a distinct asset, it is not mandatory for the right leader. Mentoring & Coaching: A deep passion for coaching and facilitating the professional development of team members. Growth Mindset and Stakeholder Management: An agile learner who stays abreast of and is effective in implementing cutting-edge technologies and trends in diagnostic imaging while being highly effective in relationship management at all levels. High Emotional Intelligence: Empathetic and solution-oriented with excellent communication skills. Financial Acumen: Strong skills in business integration, understanding financials and using data to manage financial outcomes. Logistics: Possession of a valid Driver’s License and a motor vehicle for travel between branch locations. How to Apply If this is the role for you, please apply to join this dynamic team. We thank all applicants for their interest, however, regret to advise that only short-listed candidates will be contacted. Ref: Chief Operations Officer  – Apex Apply Now

title: Service Desk Coordinator
description: Service Desk Coordinator BLEWSTREAM ECOSYSTEM Port-of-Spain / San Fernando / Trincity Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 09/02/2026 Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save We are looking for a Service Desk Coordinator to join our IT team. This role is responsible for escalating, and resolving complex and high impact incidents, across many platforms, while ensuring clear and consistent communication with stakeholders. Position Title: Service Desk Coordinator Location: 26 Bay Road, St. James, Port of Spain, 16 Orange Grove Rd, Tacarigua (Trintoplan Compound), 14- 16 Turney Street, San Fernando Employment Type: Full-Time, Hybrid Role Overview: The Service Desk Coordinator is the technical and operational owner of service delivery across the Service Desk. This role is responsible for coordinating, escalating, and resolving complex and high impact incidents, particularly across Microsoft 365, Intune, Sophos, identity, access, and endpoint management platforms, while ensuring clear, timely, and consistent communication with stakeholders. The role also provides day-to-day technical and operational guidance to junior service desk staff, supporting quality, consistency, and adherence to SLAs. Combining hands-on technical capability, service coordination, and stakeholder communication, the Service Desk Coordinator acts as the primary escalation point between the Service Desk, internal teams, external vendors, and management. Key Responsibilities: 1. Service Delivery, Incident & Escalation Management Act as the primary escalation point for complex, high-impact, or unresolved incidents. Provide 2nd and 3rd level support where required, including hands-on technical resolution. Partner with business stakeholders and end users to: understand incident impact, prioritise appropriately, and ensure quality of service and customer satisfaction Exercise sound judgment where impact, value, or risk is not immediately clear. 2. Technical Resolution & Platform Administration Perform hands-on troubleshooting and resolution for complex service desk issues that exceed 1st-line capability. Validate client and internal configurations against documented standards. Identify recurring issues and drive root-cause remediation, not just ticket closure. Support employee onboarding and access provisioning workflows where escalation or exception handling is required. 3. SLA, Queue & Priority Management Own the service desk queue, ensuring tickets are correctly logged, categorised, and prioritised, and SLAs and OLAs are actively monitored and enforced Reassign or escalate tickets as required to meet service targets. Identify SLA risks early and take corrective action. 4. Communication & Stakeholder Management Provide clear, timely updates to the organisation on major incidents, high-impact escalations and service disruptions and resolutions Liaise with client IT teams for access provisioning, application issues, and configuration dependencies. Escalate risks, recurring issues, and service gaps to the IT Manager with evidence-based recommendations. 5. Governance, Documentation & Continuous Improvement Maintain and improve knowledge base articles, escalation procedures and client-specific configuration documentation Contribute to incident reviews, problem management and service improvement initiatives Support audits, compliance requirements, and service reporting. Required Experience & Skills Experience (Essential) 3–5 years’ experience in a service desk, IT support, or service delivery role Demonstrated experience handling escalations and complex incidents Experience working with vendors and third parties Demonstrated experience overseeing day-to-day work of service desk or IT support staff, including workload coordination, escalation support, and performance feedback. Technical Skills (Essential) Strong administration and troubleshooting experience with Microsoft 365 (Entra ID, Exchange, Teams, SharePoint), Intune / endpoint management as well as endpoint security platforms (Sophos preferred) Solid understanding of incident, escalation, and service delivery practices Education Requirements (Required) Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science or Information Systems OR equivalent professional experience Preferred / Advantageous ITIL Foundation certification Microsoft certifications (e.g. M365, Azure, Endpoint Administration) Soft Skills Excellent written and verbal communication Strong ownership and accountability Strong customer service orientation Ability to translate technical issues into business-focused updates Confident decision-making under pressure Ability to prioritize and/or make judgement calls when impact/value/risk are less clear Why Join Us? Be part of a collaborative IT team focused on operational excellence. Competitive compensation, benefits, and opportunities for professional development. Hybrid work environment promoting flexibility and work-life balance. Ref: BH#458 Apply Now

title: Junior IT Associate
description:
Junior IT Associate BLEWSTREAM ECOSYSTEM Port-of-Spain / San Fernando / Trincity Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 09/02/2026 Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save We are looking for a Junior IT Associate to join our team. This role requires prior helpdesk exposure, solid Windows knowledge, and the ability to troubleshoot and resolve Level 1 application and user environment issues without constant supervision. Level 1 Incident & Request Resolution Resolve Level 1 incidents and service requests involving: Windows 10/11, Microsoft 365 applications (Outlook, Teams, Excel, Word), common desktop and line-of-business applications Install, configure, and troubleshoot applications at Level 1: Application installs and updates, user profile and configuration issues, as well as common error messages and performance issues 2. Process & Quality Follow documented procedures and escalation paths. Contribute to knowledge base articles for common fixes. Maintain professional customer service standards at all times.
